hero.question: US x Iran permanent peace deal by April 22, 2026?
hero.prob_take:
The 15.5% odds imply the crowd thinks a US-Iran permanent peace deal in 14 months is a 1-in-6 shot, a valuation that assumes structural hostility persists despite historical precedent for rapid diplomatic shifts when incentives align.

The 10-point drop in 24 hours signals panic selling rather than new information, making this a classic contrarian setup where market conviction is lowest right when geopolitical black swans tend to materialize.

daily_take.headline: Iran Peace Bet Imploding. Smart Money Sees 15% as Generous Floor.
daily_take.deck: The permanent peace deal market dropped 10 points in 24 hours on rising Middle East tensions, signaling genuine risk repricing rather than panic selling. At 15.5%, you're getting paid for a base case that requires diplomatic miracles by April 2026, but the market is probably right. The real alpha play: short this into any relief rally above 20%, because geopolitical markets have a nasty habit of front-running bad news then overcompensating on good headlines.
